business.headline: Prachar Communications' Manyavar Mohey viral campaign crosses 300 million viewsThe campaign has already generated over 8 million organic likes and has crossed 100 million cumulative views on Instagram on the celebrity collaboration post within 72 hours, marking one of the largest celebrity-led moment marketing activations in India.

Brent Crude oil hits highest since April 2024 as Middle East conflict escalatesOil markets have seen a sharp rally this week. Brent Crude has climbed about 20%, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) has surged roughly 25%.

Buying property in wife's or daughter's name? Save on stamp duty, loans and taxBuying a home is one of the biggest financial decisions many Indians make. But did you know that when a woman buys property in her own name, the purchase can come with added benefits such as lower stamp duty, cheaper home loans and useful tax deductions?

India holds sufficient crude stocks, no immediate fuel supply threat: Govt sourcesOfficials said India has sufficient stocks of petrol, diesel, LPG and natural gas. The government is closely monitoring the energy situation and reviewing it several times a day.

Sensex, Nifty end lower as Iran war lifts crude oil price; worst week in a yearThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 1,097 points to close at 78,918.90, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 315.45 points to end the day at 24,450.45.

More women homebuyers now eye Rs 90 lakh to Rs 2.5 crore homes, says ANAROCKWomen are now choosing bigger homes, targeting premium price segments, and treating property as a long-term wealth-building asset, says data.

If crude oil hits $100 per barrel, here's what it would mean for IndiaIf crude oil surges past $100 per barrel, India could face a higher import bill, a weaker rupee and rising inflation, showing how distant geopolitical conflicts can quickly ripple into fuel prices and economic stability.

Mukesh Ambani leads India's richest, Gautam Adani second: Hurun Global Rich List 2026Mukesh Ambani continues to hold the title of India's richest person and also remains Asia's wealthiest individual. His total wealth rose by about 9% over the past year to reach Rs 9.8 lakh crore.

Investing in times of war: Panic, pause or buy the dip?Sensex and Nifty have seen declines as investors reacted to rising uncertainty, volatile crude oil prices and fears of a wider conflict. For many retail investors, this has meant seeing their mutual fund or stock market portfolios slip into the red.

Building businesses that last: 5 lessons for women entrepreneursAcross the country, millions of women are running businesses in their own way. Some operate small enterprises, others are building growing ventures, but each one contributes to livelihoods, job creation, and the strength of local economies.

Sensex down 1,100 points: Why is stock market falling today?The S&P BSE Sensex was down 566.31 points at 79,449.59, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 164.85 points to 24,601.05 as of 11:24 am. Sensex and Nifty shed over half a percent.

India asks refiners to maximise LPG output, sell only to IOC, BPCL and HPCLRefiners have been directed to increase LPG output and sell the fuel only to three state-run oil marketing companies, Indian Oil Corporation, Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited.

Gulf crisis disrupts LNG supply and your kitchen may feel the heatIf LNG supply disruptions persist, the impact may soon reach your kitchen, as higher global gas prices gradually filter into household gas bills and CNG costs across Indian cities.

Sensex, Nifty in red over Iran war: When will stock market bounce back?The ongoing West Asia conflict involving Iran, Israel and the US has raised fears that energy supplies could be disrupted. If oil supply tightens, crude prices may rise further.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as Iran war weighs in on investor sentiment; RIL up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex was down 368.75 points to 79,647.15, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 115.25 points to 24,650.65 as of 9:34 am.

Gulf nations may review foreign investments as Iran war strains financesThe review could affect a wide range of financial activities. These include investment pledges to foreign governments or companies, sports sponsorship agreements, contracts with businesses and investors, and even the possible sale of certain holdings.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market rise or fall today?Gift Nifty futures were trading at 24,649 at around 8:10 am, indicating that the benchmark Nifty 50 may open below Thursday's closing level of 24,765.9.

Rs 10,000 in PPF or SIP? Which builds more wealth in 10 yearsA simple annual investment of Rs 10,000 may not sound like much, but over time it can grow into a meaningful sum. The real question is where to invest it, i.e., the predictable returns of PPF or the market-linked potential of a SIP. So which one comes out ahead after ten years? Let's have a look!

No LPG or LNG shortage, India expands talks with global energy suppliersIndia currently imports about 195 million metric standard cubic metres per day (MMSCMD) of natural gas. Out of this, around 60 MMSCMD comes from Qatar. However, authorities said India is already exploring alternative markets to reduce dependence on any single supplier.

Sensex surges 900 points, Nifty tops 24,750; Reliance jumps 3%The Sensex ended the session 900 points higher, while the Nifty closed above 24,750, as investors stepped in to buy beaten-down stocks following the recent market correction.

entertainment.headline: Rajshri Deshpande calls for open conversations on cancer: Let's normalise this Actor Rajshri Deshpande opened up about her cancer diagnosis and emphasised the need to address the stigma around the disease. The actor, now recovering after surgery, also shared her plans to raise awareness.

MasterChef India 9: Nagpur brothers Ajinkya, Vikram Gandhe take home the trophy Nagpur's Vikram and Ajinkya Gandhe won MasterChef India Season 9 by impressing judges with their blend of traditional flavours and modern presentation. The brothers became the first finalists and brought the trophy home to Maharashtra's Orange City.

Mrithyunjay review: Promising ideas galore, but convenient writing lets film downRelease Date:Fri Mar 06, 2026 Mrithyunjay movie review: Directed by Hussai Sha Kiran, Mrithyunjay is an investigative thriller that relies on the intellect of the hero and villain. While there are interesting ideas in the script, the film fails to explore its full potential.

Ash Santos on landing Marshals and learning patience in Hollywood Actor Ash Santos spoke about joining the Yellowstone universe with its spin-off, Marshals. In an exclusive interview, she recalled how rejection, a cancelled flight and a stressful screen test ultimately landed her the role of Andrea Cruz.

Project Hail Mary release pushed in India. Is it Dhurandhar 2 effect? Ryan Gosling's sci-fi film Project Hail Mary will now release in India six days after its US debut. The move is being rumoured to be an attempt to avoid a direct clash with Ranveer Singh's highly anticipated film Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Hello Bachhon review: TVF's show is less inspiration, more glorified ad campaignRelease Date:Fri Mar 06, 2026 Actor Viineet Kumar Siingh plays Physics Wallah founder Alakh Pandey in TVF's latest series on India's exam culture. Also starring Vikram Kochhar and Girija Oak, the Netflix show struggles to move beyond hero worship to tell a nuanced story.

Vijay Deverakonda's uncle opens up on actor's humility after wedding Actor Vijay Deverakonda's uncle shared photos from the actor's post-wedding hometown visit, revealing a side of the star that fans rarely see.

Badshah booked after complaint over alleged obscenity in Tateeree video Rapper-singer Badshah faces legal action after a complaint was lodged in Panchkula, accusing his new song Tateeree of promoting obscenity and disrespecting women. Investigation into the matter is underway.

BTS in India? Why 2026 seems the right time for K-pop superband's desi tour In 2026, BTS prepares to enter a new era with their latest album, ARIRANG, and the excitement, as everywhere, is at an all-time high among ARMY in India. On the other hand is India's booming live concert scenario. All things considered, this could be the right time for the Korean superband to hit the Indian stage.

Trisha shares photos after attending wedding with Vijay, thanks 'go-to people' Actor Trisha Krishnan shared solo pictures from a wedding reception in Chennai she attended with Vijay. The event comes amid public attention on Vijay's ongoing divorce proceedings after 27 years of marriage.

Vijay's bodyguard shares viral cryptic post on rumours, later deletes it After Vijay and Trisha Krishnan were seen together at a wedding reception in Chennai, speculation intensified when the actor's bodyguard shared and later deleted a cryptic Instagram post about "rising above rumours".

Ram Gopal Varma says deleted tweet on Kiara Advani bikini shot was compliment Ram Gopal Varma has finally broken his silence on the controversy surrounding his deleted tweet about Kiara Advani's bikini appearance in the War 2 teaser. The tweet had sparked significant backlash online.

Charak review: Sudipto Sen's film explores devotion that often blurs humanityRelease Date:Fri Mar 06, 2026 Directed by Shieladitya Moulik and produced by Sudipto Sen, Charak explores blind faith during a ritualistic festival. Sahidur Rahaman and Anjali Patil anchor the unsettling thriller with powerful performances.

Vijay calls Trisha 'princess', she says he is 'home' in old viral videos. Watch An old video of Vijay and Trisha Krishnan is going viral again after the two were spotted together at a wedding reception. In the clip, Vijay calls Trisha a "princess," while she says he feels like "home" to her.

Boyfriend On Demand review: Jisoo and Seo In-guk make this AI romance watchableRelease Date:Fri Mar 06, 2026 Boyfriend On Demand, starring BLACKPINK's Jisoo and Seo In-guk, explores the idea of AI-driven romance. While the series features engaging performances and fun cameos, it takes time to build the emotional depth expected from a romantic comedy.

Govinda on his controversial image in Bollywood: Everyone is given a bad name Govinda addressed the controversies linked to his name, saying that every actor in the film industry goes through phases of criticism and reputation damage.

Hoppers review: A wildly inventive Pixar adventure with plenty of heartRelease Date:Fri Mar 06, 2026 Pixar's Hoppers review: Daniel Chong's animated adventure blends wild comedy, emotional storytelling and environmental themes in a heartfelt tale about a teenager who becomes a robotic beaver to save a forest.

Anurag Dobhal's brother on torture accusations: He filed a fake case against us Bigg Boss 17 contestant Anurag Dobhal's brother Kalam broke silence on the accusations of torture the YouTuber has brought against his family. In a statement on social media, Kalam shared that his brother has filed a fake case against them.

Mona Singh says brotherhood culture everywhere makes women feel like outsiders Mona Singh shed light on real-life gender bias reflected in Kohrra 2. In a recent interview, the actor addressed the "brotherhood romance" in workplaces and other traditional attitudes that affect women.

general news.headline: Days before US sank warship, Iran sought docking for another vessel in KochiDays before a US submarine sank IRIS Dena in the Indian Ocean off the coast of Sri Lanka, India gave safe harbour to another Iranian frigate. According to government sources, the ship's crew has been put up at naval quarters in Kochi.

No deal except unconditional surrender: Trump's message for Iran as war deepensTrump's call for Iran's complete surrender comes nearly a week after the US and Israel launched coordinated strikes against Islamic republic, triggering retaliatory missile and drone attacks across the Middle East and raising fears of a wider war.

A week into the US-Israel-Iran war: The impact and who stands whereUS-Israel's war on Iran entered the seventh day on Friday as missiles continued to criss-cross across the Middle East, unlocking multiple fronts in the conflict. The war has left over 1,200 dead in Iran, with no signs of slowing down. How did the war escalate? What has been its impact so far? How far will it go?

Who is Balen Shah, a Maithili-speaking rapper, likely to be new Nepal PM?Ex-rapper and Kathmandu's former mayor, Balen Shah, is all set to become Nepal's next prime minister. A Maithili-speaking leader with a fierce Madhesi identity, his rise in Nepali politics would bring a major shift from the old political guard. As the Mayor, Balen Shah transformed Kathmandu.

He made Pak nuclear power, but Pak bombed his memoryPakistan's only Nobel laureate, Abdus Salam, helped build the foundations of the country's nuclear programme. Because he belonged to the Ahmadiyya sect, his religious identity was stripped away, and his legacy was erased in Pakistan. Islamabad boasts of its nuclear weapons, but gives no credit to Salam.

I celebrate every success with aloo chaat: UPSC topper Anuj AgnihotriThe UPSC 2025 topper, Anuj Agnihotri, said he loves celebrating every success with aloo chaat. He also spoke about his joy, family support and the long preparation that carried him from medical studies to the top rank of India's one of the toughest exams.

Russia providing Iran with intel to strike US military infra across Gulf: ReportAfter the US and Israel launched joint strikes on Iran last weekend, Tehran's wave of retaliatory attacks across the Middle East targeted military bases, embassies and refineries. According to a Washington Post report, Iran's precise strikes were guided by Russian intelligence.

Flipkart lays off hundreds of employees after annual performance reviewFlipkart has reportedly asked hundreds of employees to leave following its annual performance review cycle, with estimates suggesting 300 to 500 workers were affected. The move comes as the e-commerce company wants operational discipline while preparing for a potential IPO.

India, this is the man you need to thank for discovering Jasprit BumrahT20 World Cup: Jasprit Bumrah bowled yet another riveting spell to take India through to the T20 World Cup final. India Today traced the man who discovered Bumrah, and turned him into the bowler he is today.

Live: Iran targets US bases in UAE, Kuwait; Hezbollah launches drone attack on IsraelUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: On Day 7, the war in the Middle East intensified as Iran launched fresh missile attacks on Israel and US bases, with Israel intercepting incoming strikes. Israeli forces also hit Hezbollah positions in Lebanon, where at least eight people were killed. The conflict has killed more than 1,230 people in Iran, over 70 in Lebanon and about a dozen in Israel, while six U.S. troops have died. The U.S. Embassy in Kuwait shut down after Iranian retaliatory strikes. The fighting has rattled global markets, pushed oil prices higher and slowed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, while U.S. President Donald Trump signalled interest in influencing Iran's next leadership. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for live updates on the ongoing Middle East conflict.

Vijay, Trisha attend wedding together days after wife alleges affair with actorVijay and Trisha Krishnan attended the wedding reception of Kalpathi Suresh and Meenakshi Suresh's son. This is their first appearance amid the Jana Nayagan actor's divorce case.

Live: Balen Shah's party heads for landslide victory in Nepal electionNepal Election Results 2026 Live Updates: Vote counting is underway in Nepal's high-stakes general election in which rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah's party gained leads in early counting. Out of a total of 275 seats in Nepal's lower house of parliament, counting is in progress for 165 seats that are elected by direct election. The remaining 110 seats will be filled later via a system of proportional representation. Voting took place on Thursday, months after a Gen Z-led protest movement forced Oli and his Communist Party of Nepal (Unified Marxist–Leninist) government out of power in September 2025. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for the latest updates.

What is Blue Sparrow? The 'missile from space' that killed KhameneiIsrael reportedly used the Blue Sparrow, a missile that briefly exits the atmosphere before falling almost vertically onto its target, to kill Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Tehran on February 28, 2026. Here is the science behind the weapon.

How Robotic Knee Replacement is Redefining the Journey Back to MobilityEarly consultation with an orthopaedic specialist is essential. Delaying surgery in severe cases can lead to worsening deformity and reduced functional outcomes.

Rain and snow likely in hills on Saturday, Delhi to have clear skiesIndia faces early summer as heatwaves grip Mumbai and parts of Maharashtra. Check the March 7 weather forecast for Delhi and Gujarat, and whether it will rain in the Himalayan region.

Zionists made big mistake: First post from Khamenei's X account after his deathIn the first post on Ali Khamenei's X account since his death, Iran sent a warning to Israel. The post contained an AI-generated picture, depicting the manufacturing of missiles and hitting the targets.

How the India-Canada nuclear pact exorcises the ghost of TrudeausIndia and Canada are moving to reset ties through a landmark civil nuclear agreement, decades after relations were ruptured by India's use of a Canadian reactor for the 1974 Pokhran nuclear test. The pact also closes a turbulent chapter shaped by Canadian PMs Pierre Trudeau and his son Justin.

Iran war isn't ending soon. And it could threaten Trump's presidencyTwo days after the US-Israeli strike killed Ali Khamenei, sending Iran into an all-out war, Donald Trump said the conflict was expected to last four to five weeks. But reports now suggest his administration was unprepared for the broader war it faces. The conflict could stretch to September and threaten Trump's presidency.

Your online payments are safe behind powerful maths. But for how long?Every time you pay online, your data is protected by a maths problem nobody has proved is unbreakable. Here is what that means, and why the world is already preparing for the worst.

Andhra CM announces Rs 25,000 incentive for 2nd, 3rd child to lift fertility rateThe government plans to introduce the population management policy from April 1. It includes incentives such as 25,000 for the birth of a second or third child, along with monthly financial support of 1,000 for the third child for five years and free education up to 18 years.

The grammar of counter-terrorism: What Prahaar really signalsPrahaar codifies India's counterterrorism doctrine, prioritising intelligence-led disruption, legal rigor, uniform state capacities, tech-enabled surveillance, and international coordination to pre-empt, deter, and prosecute threats.

I'll come to Bihar only: Nitish Kumar to party amid anger over Rajya Sabha moveJD(U)'s internal polls come a day after Nitish Kumar filed his nomination for the Rajya Sabha, a move that marks a major shift in Bihar politics and leaves a power void in his party.

Why Trump doesn't want India to become rival like ChinaHandcrafted ByOur Editors The US seems to have suddenly woken up to the 'mistakes' it made while dealing with China 20 years ago. It resulted in China being the economic powerhouse it is today. Now, Trump wants to avoid repeating those with India. But India never needed anyone for its rise.

Man returns to Delhi from Dubai amid Iran war, internet gushes over his 'face card'An interview of a passenger arriving in Delhi from Dubai amid escalating Middle East tensions has gone viral for an unexpected reason, with social media users more focused on the man's striking looks than the interview itself.

sports.headline: Twelve ISL clubs accuse AIFF of profiting from 'crisis-hit season' Twelve ISL clubs wrote to the AIFF, alleging that the federation is set to earn a surplus of over Rs 3.4 crore from the ongoing season despite assuring the Sports Ministry it would not "make a rupee" from the league this year.

Glenn Phillips hopes New Zealand face a 'human' Jasprit Bumrah in final Jasprit Bumrah is a world-class operator, but even he can have an off day, something Glenn Phillips and New Zealand will be hoping for when they face India in the T20 World Cup final on Sunday.

I was nervous: Shivam Dube opens up on bowling nerve-wracking last over in semis T20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Shivam Dube admitted he was nervous while bowling the final over against England, where he had to defend 30 runs, but said he backed his abilities as India won by seven runs to secure their place in the final.

WI remain stranded in Kolkata, four SA players likely to leave for New Zealand West Indies players have been stranded in Kolkata for the fifth consecutive day after their exit from the T20 World Cup, as international airspace restrictions triggered by US and Israeli strikes on Iran continue to disrupt travel plans across the region.

Cristiano Ronaldo flies to Madrid for treatment as injury concerns deepen Jorge Jesus, the head coach of Al-Nassr, has revealed that Cristiano Ronaldo's hamstring injury is more serious than initially expected, with the Portuguese star travelling to Madrid to undergo specialist treatment.

India Women chip away, but Australia stay ahead on pace-heavy day in Perth Test INDW vs AUSW: Australia had a steady opening day and trail by 102 runs with seven wickets in hand in the pink-ball Test. However, India will feel they remain in the contest, with the WACA pitch offering significant assistance to the fast bowlers.

Arvid Lindblad brings F1 excitement back to India at Red Bull Motojam 2026: In Photos From drifting to stunt biking to an F1 car on Indian soil, Red Bull Moto Jam 2026 in Noida was a motorsport spectacle like no other ahead of the new F1 season.

India arrive in Ahmedabad as history beckons at T20 World Cup final IND vs NZ, T20 World Cup 2026 Final: India arrived in Ahmedabad ahead of the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand at the Narendra Modi Stadium, chasing history as they bid to become the first team to win back-to-back titles.

Kapil Dev backs out-of-form Abhishek Sharma to play T20 World Cup final Kapil Dev said the Indian team management should retain Abhishek Sharma for the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand despite his struggles in the tournament so far.

Jasprit Bumrah is the 'best of all time': England's Brook after semi-final loss T20 World Cup: England captain Harry Brook hailed star Indian pacer Jasprit Bumrah as the best of all time after India's nail-biting seven-run win in the T20 World Cup semi-final clash at the Wankhede Stadium.

ICC names officials for India vs New Zealand T20 World Cup finalThe International Cricket Council has appointed Richard Illingworth and Alex Wharf as on-field umpires for the T20 World Cup 2026 final between Suryakumar Yadav's India and Mitchell Santner's New Zealand in Ahmedabad on Sunday.

Sanju saving his best for final: India star's childhood coach makes bold predictionT20 World Cup 2026: Sanju Samson's childhood coach George believes the wicketkeeper-batter has saved his best for the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand in Ahmedabad, backing him to continue his stellar form.

Jasprit Bumrah in a playing XI is like having a genie, a superpower: Faf du PlessisT20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Faf du Plessis lauded Jasprit Bumrah for his impressive spell against England on Thursday at the Wankhede Stadium, which helped India qualify for the final against New Zealand on Sunday.

Leeds boss Farke handed one-match touchline ban after Man City defeatLeeds United manager Daniel Farke was handed a one-match ban and fined £8,000 for confronting match officials after the club's Premier League defeat to Manchester City.

T20 World Cup final fever: Western Railways add 2 special trains to AhmedabadWith the T20 World Cup final in mind, the Western Railways has decided to add two special superfast trains between Mumbai Central and Ahmedabad. India will take on New Zealand in the final on Sunday, March 8.

Has Buttler fallen out of love playing for England? Ex England pacer thinks soAs Jos Buttler reflects on a challenging T20 World Cup, former teammates and observers say the England veteran must decide if he has the desire to continue playing international cricket.

Alyssa Healy's nephew, Mitchell Starc accompany Australia star for farewell TestINDW vs AUSW: Alyssa Healy bid an emotional farewell in Perth as her nephew joined her during the national anthem before her final Test for Australia, with Mitchell Starc also present at the WACA.

Lakshya Sen takes on Li Shi Feng in high-stakes All England Open quarter-finalLakshya Sen stands as India's last hope at the All England Open as he faces world No. 6 Li Shi Feng in the quarter-final in Birmingham. The clash revives a long-standing rivalry that is perfectly balanced at 7-7.

Sanju Samson becoming India's batting leader amid Abhishek's struggles: Irfan PathanT20 World Cup: Irfan Pathan has said that Sanju Samson has become India's batting leader amid Abhishek Sharma's struggles with the bat during the T20 World Cup 2026. Samson scored 89 to help India beat England in the semi-final in Mumbai.

Axar Patel filling void left by Jadeja, says Gavaskar after semifinal heroicsT20 World Cup 2026: Sunil Gavaskar has predicted Axar Patel will become one of India's greatest players, backing him to fill the void left by Ravindra Jadeja after his stunning semifinal display against England.

Axar Patel's fielding vs England like Kapil Dev in 1983 World Cup final: SrikkanthT20 World Cup: Kris Srikkanth claimed that Axar Patel's catch to dismiss Harry Brook reminded him of the superhuman effort that Kapil Dev produced in the 1983 World Cup final to dismiss Viv Richards.

Dropped at start, Sanju Samson storms into T20 World Cup Player of the Tournament raceT20 World Cup: Sanju Samson has been shortlisted for the ICC T20 World Cup Player of the Tournament despite playing only four matches. The India opener has scored 232 runs at a strike rate of 201.73, emerging as one of the tournament's biggest game-changers.

Dravid and Ashwin set for new role in T20 cricket as franchise owners in EuropeRahul Dravid and Ravichandran Ashwin are reportedly set to become franchise owners in the European T20 Premier League, with an Indian-based consortium agreed to acquire a Glasgow-based franchise.

What's changed with Samson? Shastri explains his rise in form in T20 World Cup 2026Ravi Shastri says Sanju Samson has finally toughened up mentally, backing his strengths and being wiser with shot selection to produce match-winning form at the T20 World Cup 2026.

If you are backing Abhishek Sharma, do the same with Varun Chakravarthy: SehwagT20 World Cup semi-final: Virender Sehwag feels that India should continue to back Varun Chakravarthy just like they have been doing the same with Abhishek Sharma throughout the duration of the T20 World Cup 2026.

Messi's Donald Trump visit turns awkward as US President mentions RonaldoLionel Messi's White House visit took an unexpected turn when Donald Trump mentioned Cristiano Ronaldo during his speech. The moment quickly went viral, with fans amused by Messi's reaction during the MLS Cup celebration.

Should England continue with McCullum as head coach? Harry Brook shares his thoughtsT20 World Cup 2026: England captain Harry Brook has given Brendan McCullum a "125%" vote of confidence despite a T20 World Cup semi-final exit, backing his coach to stay on despite pressure from the Ashes defeat.

India bowlers need to learn from Jasprit Bumrah: Sunil Gavaskar ahead of finalT20 World Cup: Indian cricket legend Sunil Gavaksar, speaking exclusively to India Today, emphasised how the rest of the India bowling unit need to learn and try to replicate the clinical brilliance of their pace spearhead Jasprit Bumrah.

India's unshakable team spirit stood tall in Wankhede's run festT20 World Cup semi-final: On a testing night against England, India's collective team spirit eventually paved the way for their narrow seven-run victory in the semi-final of the T20 World Cup 2026. The team's ability to respond collectively when the pressure peaked stood out in the nerve-wracking run fest.

Sanju Samson was on a different planet: Sunil Gavaskar hails heroics vs EnglandT20 World Cup: Sanju Samson's stunning knock against England left Sunil Gavaskar in awe, with the legend saying the India opener looked like he was batting on a different planet. His brilliance powered India into the T20 World Cup final.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
